steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: The coefficients $13$, $-13$, and $65$ share a factor of $13$. Each term also contains at least one factor of $x$, so the greatest common factor is $13x$.

steps.title: Factor each term
steps.content:
Divide each term by $13x$. This removes the largest factor common to all three terms:
$$
13x^{10}=13x\cdot x^9
$$
$$
-13x^9=13x\cdot(-x^8)
$$
$$
65x=13x\cdot 5
$$
